title: Laser capture microdissection (LCM) and RNA isolation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"od3qm","text":"Tissue samples for RNA analysis were obtained from brain with Laser Capture Microdissection System  (Arcturus).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Tissue samples from 12 μm-thick frozen sections for RNA analysis were obtained from each hypothalamic nucleus or 50 captured GFP-positive or -negative cells onto a single CapSure HS LCM Cap (Arcturus Engineering, Mountain View, CA) using the following parameters: spot size = 7.5 μm; power = 50–80 mW; and pulse duration = 50–1,000 ms. Total RNAs were isolated by PicoPure RNA Isolation Kit (Arcturus Engineering), and treated with DNase I (Invitrogen).
The authors declare no competing financial interests.

Haematopoietic cells produce BDNF and regulate appetite upon migration to the hypothalamus by Hiroshi Urabe, Hideto Kojima, Lawrence Chan, Tomoya Terashima, Nobuhiro Ogawa, Miwako Katagi, Kazunori Fujino, Asako Kumagai, Hiromichi Kawai, Akihiro Asakawa, Akio Inui, Hitoshi Yasuda, Yutaka Eguchi, Kazuhiro Oka, Hiroshi Maegawa, Atsunori Kashiwagi, an published in Nature Communications on 04 Mar, 2013 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ncomms2536
creator: Protocols Importer
